Replies: A password is a secret string used to secure your accounts. It should be strong and unique.
Use strong passwords with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols. Avoid reusing passwords.
Never share your login details with anyone, even if they claim to be support staff.
Weak passwords like 123456 or 'password' are easy to crack. Always use strong, unique passwords.
Avoid predictable passwords such as names, birthdays, or common words.
Phishing is a cyber attack where criminals trick you into revealing personal information through fake emails or websites.
Always check the sender's email address and avoid clicking suspicious links.
Phishing messages often create urgency to pressure you into acting quickly.
A scam is a deceptive trick used by cybercriminals to steal money or personal information.
Scammers often pretend to be trusted organisations to gain your trust.
If something feels suspicious online, trust your instincts and verify it.
Malware is harmful software designed to damage or steal data from your device.
It can spread through downloads, email attachments, or unsafe websites.
Use antivirus software and avoid downloading files from unknown sources.
Ransomware locks your files and demands payment to restore access.
Always keep backups of your important data to protect yourself.
Never pay the ransom — there is no guarantee your files will be restored.
Spyware secretly monitors your activity without your permission.
It can steal personal data like passwords and browsing history.
A keylogger records everything you type, including passwords and messages.
A hacker is someone who tries to gain unauthorized access to systems.
Ethical hackers help improve security by finding vulnerabilities.
Ethical hacking is legal testing of systems to find and fix security weaknesses.
A firewall acts as a barrier between your device and unwanted network traffic.
Antivirus software detects and removes malware from your device.
A VPN encrypts your internet connection and protects your privacy online.
Encryption converts data into unreadable form that only authorized users can decode.
Decryption is the process of turning encrypted data back into readable form.
Two-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security beyond your password.
Even if someone steals your password, 2FA helps protect your account.
Authentication verifies your identity before allowing access to an account.
Authorization determines what actions you are allowed to perform in a system.
A data breach happens when sensitive data is accessed without permission.
Identity theft is when someone steals your personal information to commit fraud.
Protect your privacy by limiting the personal information you share online.
Review app permissions and social media settings regularly.
Social engineering manipulates people into revealing confidential information.
Spam messages are unwanted emails often used for scams or malware.
A DDoS attack floods a system with traffic to make it unavailable.
A botnet is a group of infected devices controlled by hackers.
Brute force attacks try many password combinations until one works.
Always update your software to fix security vulnerabilities.
Security patches fix vulnerabilities in software systems.
Cloud security protects data stored online using encryption and access control.
Avoid using public Wi-Fi for sensitive activities unless using a VPN.
Always check for HTTPS and a padlock icon before entering sensitive data.
HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and websites.
Cookies store information about your browsing activity.
The dark web is a hidden part of the internet often used anonymously.
A cyber attack is an attempt to damage or gain unauthorized access to systems.
A threat is any potential danger to a system.
A vulnerability is a weakness that can be exploited by attackers.
Risk is the chance of loss or damage from a security issue.
Always back up your data regularly to prevent data loss.
Incident response is the process of handling cybersecurity breaches.
